title: centaurea ambigua subsp. laciniata
intro: Centaurea ambigua subsp. laciniata, also known as the cut-leaf knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of the western Mediterranean and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Centaurea ambigua subsp. laciniata can be consumed as tea, infused in oil for topical use, or taken as a supplement in capsule form, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
